# Project Scaffolding Examples

This guide demonstrates how Codestral can build complete projects from scratch using its agentic capabilities.

## 🏗️ Complete Project Creation

### Static Website Portfolio

```bash
> Build me a professional portfolio website with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Include sections for about, projects, skills, and contact with a responsive design.

# AI will create:
# ├── index.html (Homepage with navigation)
# ├── about.html (About page with bio)
# ├── projects.html (Project showcase)
# ├── contact.html (Contact form)
# ├── css/
# │   ├── style.css (Main styles)
# │   ├── responsive.css (Mobile styles)
# │   └── animations.css (CSS animations)
# ├── js/
# │   ├── main.js (Navigation and interactions)
# │   ├── projects.js (Project data and filtering)
# │   └── contact.js (Form validation)
# ├── assets/
# │   └── README.md (Instructions for adding images)
# └── README.md (Setup and customization guide)

Expected features:
- Responsive design (mobile-first)
- Smooth scrolling navigation
- Project filtering and modal views
- Contact form with validation
- CSS animations and transitions
- SEO-friendly structure
```

### React Todo Application

```bash
> Create a complete React todo application with local storage, filtering, drag-and-drop, and dark mode.

# AI will create:
# ├── package.json (React dependencies)
# ├── public/
# │   ├── index.html
# │   └── manifest.json
# ├── src/
# │   ├── App.js (Main component)
# │   ├── App.css (Global styles)
# │   ├── components/
# │   │   ├── TodoList.jsx
# │   │   ├── TodoItem.jsx
# │   │   ├── AddTodo.jsx
# │   │   ├── FilterButtons.jsx
# │   │   └── ThemeToggle.jsx
# │   ├── hooks/
# │   │   ├── useTodos.js
# │   │   ├── useLocalStorage.js
# │   │   └── useTheme.js
# │   ├── utils/
# │   │   └── todoHelpers.js
# │   └── styles/
# │       ├── components.css
# │       └── themes.css
# └── README.md

Expected features:
- Add, edit, delete, complete todos
- Filter by all/active/completed
- Drag and drop reordering
- Local storage persistence
- Dark/light theme toggle
- Responsive design
```

### Express REST API

```bash
> Build a Node.js REST API for a blog platform with authentication, CRUD operations, file uploads, and MongoDB integration.

# AI will create:
# ├── package.json (Express, MongoDB, JWT dependencies)
# ├── server.js (Main server file)
# ├── .env.example (Environment variables template)
# ├── src/
# │   ├── config/
# │   │   ├── database.js (MongoDB connection)
# │   │   └── cloudinary.js (File upload config)
# │   ├── models/
# │   │   ├── User.js (User schema)
# │   │   ├── Post.js (Blog post schema)
# │   │   └── Comment.js (Comment schema)
# │   ├── routes/
# │   │   ├── auth.js (Authentication routes)
# │   │   ├── posts.js (Blog post CRUD)
# │   │   ├── users.js (User management)
# │   │   └── upload.js (File upload routes)
# │   ├── middleware/
# │   │   ├── auth.js (JWT verification)
# │   │   ├── validation.js (Input validation)
# │   │   ├── upload.js (Multer configuration)
# │   │   └── errorHandler.js (Error handling)
# │   ├── controllers/
# │   │   ├── authController.js
# │   │   ├── postController.js
# │   │   └── userController.js
# │   └── utils/
# │       ├── jwt.js (Token utilities)
# │       └── email.js (Email service)
# ├── tests/
# │   ├── auth.test.js
# │   ├── posts.test.js
# │   └── setup.js
# └── README.md

Expected features:
- User registration and login
- JWT authentication
- Blog post CRUD operations
- File upload with Cloudinary
- Input validation and sanitization
- Error handling and logging
- API documentation
- Unit and integration tests
```

### Full-Stack E-commerce

```bash
> Create a complete e-commerce application with React frontend, Node.js backend, payment processing, and admin dashboard.

# AI will create a comprehensive project structure:
# ├── client/ (React frontend)
# │   ├── package.json
# │   ├── src/
# │   │   ├── components/
# │   │   │   ├── common/ (Header, Footer, Loading)
# │   │   │   ├── product/ (ProductCard, ProductList, ProductDetail)
# │   │   │   ├── cart/ (Cart, CartItem, Checkout)
# │   │   │   ├── user/ (Login, Register, Profile)
# │   │   │   └── admin/ (Dashboard, ProductManager, OrderManager)
# │   │   ├── pages/
# │   │   │   ├── Home.jsx
# │   │   │   ├── Products.jsx
# │   │   │   ├── ProductDetail.jsx
# │   │   │   ├── Cart.jsx
# │   │   │   ├── Checkout.jsx
# │   │   │   └── Admin.jsx
# │   │   ├── hooks/
# │   │   │   ├── useAuth.js
# │   │   │   ├── useCart.js
# │   │   │   └── useProducts.js
# │   │   ├── context/
# │   │   │   ├── AuthContext.js
# │   │   │   └── CartContext.js
# │   │   └── services/
# │   │       ├── api.js
# │   │       ├── auth.js
# │   │       └── payment.js
# ├── server/ (Node.js backend)
# │   ├── package.json
# │   ├── src/
# │   │   ├── models/ (User, Product, Order, Cart)
# │   │   ├── routes/ (auth, products, orders, payments)
# │   │   ├── controllers/ (Business logic)
# │   │   ├── middleware/ (Auth, validation, CORS)
# │   │   └── services/ (Stripe, email, inventory)
# ├── shared/ (Common utilities and types)
# └── README.md

Expected features:
- Product catalog with search and filtering
- Shopping cart and wishlist
- User authentication and profiles
- Stripe payment integration
- Order management system
- Admin dashboard for inventory
- Responsive design
- Email notifications
- Inventory tracking
```

## 🎨 Frontend Frameworks

### Vue.js Application

```bash
> Build a Vue.js task management application with Vuex for state management and Vue Router for navigation.

# AI creates Vue project with:
# - Vue 3 with Composition API
# - Vuex store for state management
# - Vue Router for navigation
# - Component library (Vuetify or similar)
# - Task CRUD operations
# - User authentication
# - Responsive design
```

### Next.js Blog

```bash
> Create a Next.js blog with static generation, MDX support, and a CMS integration.

# AI creates Next.js project with:
# - Static site generation (SSG)
# - MDX for blog posts
# - Contentful or Strapi CMS
# - SEO optimization
# - Image optimization
# - Dark mode support
# - Comment system
```

### Angular Dashboard

```bash
> Build an Angular admin dashboard with charts, data tables, and real-time updates.

# AI creates Angular project with:
# - Angular Material UI
# - Chart.js integration
# - Data tables with sorting/filtering
# - WebSocket for real-time updates
# - Lazy loading modules
# - Authentication guards
# - Responsive layout
```

## 🗄️ Backend Services

### GraphQL API

```bash
> Create a GraphQL API with Apollo Server, authentication, and real-time subscriptions.

# AI creates:
# - Apollo Server setup
# - GraphQL schema definitions
# - Resolvers for queries/mutations
# - JWT authentication
# - Real-time subscriptions
# - DataLoader for optimization
# - GraphQL Playground
```

### Microservices Architecture

```bash
> Build a microservices architecture for a social media platform with user service, post service, and notification service.

# AI creates:
# - Multiple service directories
# - Docker containers for each service
# - API Gateway with Express
# - Inter-service communication
# - Shared database models
# - Message queue (Redis/RabbitMQ)
# - Service discovery
# - Load balancing configuration
```

### Serverless Functions

```bash
> Create serverless functions for image processing, email sending, and data analytics using AWS Lambda.

# AI creates:
# - Serverless framework configuration
# - Lambda function handlers
# - AWS SDK integrations
# - Environment configuration
# - Deployment scripts
# - CloudFormation templates
# - API Gateway integration
```

```bash
# - Horizontal Pod Autoscaler
# - Monitoring with Prometheus
# - Logging with ELK stack

# - Security policies
```

```bash
# - Contract deployment scripts
```

## 📝 Documentation and Guides

### Project Documentation

```bash
> Generate comprehensive documentation for my project including API docs, user guides, and developer setup.

# AI creates:
# - README with setup instructions
# - API documentation (OpenAPI/Swagger)
# - User guide with screenshots
# - Developer documentation
# - Contributing guidelines
# - Code of conduct
# - License file
# - Changelog
```

## 🔧 Customization Options

### Template Customization

```bash
# Specify technology preferences
> Build a React app using TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Zustand for state management

# Request specific features
> Create an e-commerce site with Stripe payments, inventory management, and admin dashboard

# Specify architecture patterns
> Build a Node.js API using clean architecture with dependency injection and CQRS pattern

# Request performance optimizations
> Create a high-performance React app with code splitting, lazy loading, and service workers
```

### Integration Requirements

```bash
# Database preferences
> Build a blog API using PostgreSQL with Prisma ORM

# Authentication providers
> Create a social app with Google, Facebook, and GitHub OAuth

# Payment processors
> Build an e-commerce platform with Stripe and PayPal integration

# Cloud services
> Create a file sharing app using AWS S3 and CloudFront
```


## 🚀 Best Practices for Project Scaffolding

### Effective Project Requests

```bash
# ✅ Good - Specific requirements
> Build a React e-commerce app with Stripe payments, product search, shopping cart, and admin dashboard

# ✅ Good - Technology preferences
> Create a Node.js REST API using Express, MongoDB, JWT authentication, and comprehensive testing

# ✅ Good - Feature specifications
> Build a task management app with drag-and-drop, real-time collaboration, file attachments, and mobile support

# ❌ Avoid - Too vague
> Make me a website

# ❌ Avoid - Conflicting requirements
> Build a simple app with 50 advanced features
```

### Project Planning

```bash
# Let AI plan first
> Can you create a project plan for a social media platform before we start building?

# Review and approve
> The plan looks good, but can you add real-time notifications and video upload features?

# Start implementation
> Great! Now let's build it step by step starting with the backend API
```

### Iterative Development

```bash
# Start with MVP
> Build a basic blog with posts and comments first

# Add features incrementally  
> Now add user authentication and user profiles

# Enhance with advanced features
> Add rich text editor, image uploads, and social sharing

# Polish and optimize
> Add SEO optimization, performance improvements, and analytics
```
